_meta.purpose: Business logic definitions injected into AI prompt context. The AI must use these exact SQL expressions when the user references these concepts.
_meta.note: All status/segment/plan comparisons use LOWER() — raw data has inconsistent casing.

churned_customer.label: Churned Customer
churned_customer.definition: A customer who has placed no orders in the last 90 days
churned_customer.sql: id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT customer_id FROM orders WHERE order_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days' AND customer_id IS NOT NULL)

active_customer.label: Active Customer
active_customer.definition: A customer who placed at least one order in the last 90 days
active_customer.sql: id IN (SELECT DISTINCT customer_id FROM orders WHERE order_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days' AND customer_id IS NOT NULL)

at_risk_customer.label: At-Risk Customer
at_risk_customer.definition: Previously active but no order in 45–90 days — likely to churn soon
at_risk_customer.sql: id IN (SELECT DISTINCT customer_id FROM orders WHERE order_date < CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '45 days' AND order_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days')

high_value_customer.label: High-Value Customer
high_value_customer.definition: Customer with total completed revenue greater than $500

whale_customer.label: Whale Customer
whale_customer.definition: Top-tier customer with lifetime value above $5,000 — VIP treatment required

new_customer.label: New Customer
new_customer.definition: Customer who signed up in the last 30 days

mrr.label: MRR — Monthly Recurring Revenue
mrr.definition: Sum of all completed order amounts in the current calendar month
mrr.sql: SELECT SUM(amount) AS mrr FROM orders WHERE LOWER(status) = 'completed' AND DATE_TRUNC('month', order_date) = DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)

arr.label: ARR — Annual Recurring Revenue
arr.sql: SELECT SUM(amount) * 12 AS arr FROM orders WHERE LOWER(status) = 'completed' AND DATE_TRUNC('month', order_date) = DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)

revenue_growth.label: Revenue Growth (MoM)
revenue_growth.definition: Month-over-month percentage change in completed order revenue
revenue_growth.sql: WITH m AS (SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month',order_date) mo, SUM(amount) rev FROM orders WHERE LOWER(status)='completed' GROUP BY 1) SELECT mo, rev, LAG(rev) OVER (ORDER BY mo) prev_rev, ROUND((rev - LAG(rev) OVER (ORDER BY mo)) / NULLIF(LAG(rev) OVER (ORDER BY mo),0)*100,2) AS growth_pct FROM m ORDER BY mo

aov.label: AOV — Average Order Value
aov.definition: Total completed revenue divided by number of completed orders
aov.sql: SELECT ROUND(SUM(amount)::NUMERIC / NULLIF(COUNT(*),0), 2) AS aov FROM orders WHERE LOWER(status) = 'completed'

net_revenue.label: Net Revenue
net_revenue.definition: Gross revenue from completed orders minus payment gateway fees
net_revenue.sql: SELECT SUM(o.amount) - COALESCE(SUM(p.gateway_fee),0) AS net_revenue FROM orders o LEFT JOIN payments p ON p.order_id = o.id WHERE LOWER(o.status) = 'completed'

conversion_rate.label: Conversion Rate
conversion_rate.definition: Percentage of all orders with status = completed
conversion_rate.sql: SELECT ROUND(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE LOWER(status)='completed')::NUMERIC / NULLIF(COUNT(*),0) * 100, 2) AS conversion_rate_pct FROM orders

refund_rate.label: Refund Rate
refund_rate.definition: Percentage of completed payments that resulted in a refund
refund_rate.sql: SELECT ROUND(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE LOWER(status)='refunded')::NUMERIC / NULLIF(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE LOWER(status)='completed'),0) * 100, 2) AS refund_rate_pct FROM payments

payment_failure_rate.label: Payment Failure Rate
payment_failure_rate.definition: Percentage of payment attempts that failed
payment_failure_rate.sql: SELECT ROUND(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE LOWER(status)='failed')::NUMERIC / NULLIF(COUNT(*),0) * 100, 2) AS failure_rate_pct FROM payments

time_windows.wow: Compare DATE_TRUNC('week', CURRENT_DATE) vs DATE_TRUNC('week', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '1 week'
time_windows.mom: Compare DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) vs DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '1 month'
time_windows.yoy: Compare EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE) vs EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE) - 1

data_quality_rules.status_normalization: Always use LOWER(status) for comparisons — raw data has Active, ACTIVE, active all meaning the same
data_quality_rules.region_normalization: Always use LOWER(region) for comparisons — raw data has Europe, EUROPE, europe
data_quality_rules.plan_normalization: Always use LOWER(plan) for comparisons — raw data has Pro, PRO, pro
data_quality_rules.always_use: COALESCE(gateway_fee, 0) when summing fees; NULLIF(denominator, 0) to avoid division by zero

schema_summary.customers: id, first_name, last_name, email, phone(nullable), region, segment, plan, status, signup_date, lifetime_value
schema_summary.orders: id, customer_id(fk→customers), order_date, product_name(nullable), quantity, amount, discount_pct, status
schema_summary.payments: id, order_id(fk→orders), method, status, payment_date, amount, gateway_fee(nullable)
